title: Microskill config
description: Runtime configuration for a microskill. Profile YAMLs live under <skill>/profiles/<profile>.yaml. The base profile (profiles/base.yaml) is always loaded; overlays deep-merge over it. Closed grammar: unknown fields at any level are rejected.

version.description: Schema version. Only 1 is recognized. Omitting it defaults to 1 (set by resolve-microskill before validation); an explicit value other than 1 still blocks.

profile.description: Profile routing metadata. Allowed only in profiles/base.yaml; rejected in overlays at resolve time (semantic, not schema-enforced).

default.description: Name of the profile to use when the caller does not pass --profile. Must resolve to a file profiles/<value>.yaml. Absent means base.yaml itself is the effective profile.

vars.description: Flat map of template variable substitutions. Every key becomes {{key}} in SKILL.md prose and in generated output.

inputs.description: Customize entries from the skill's Inputs table. Keys are input names declared in SKILL.md.

required.description: When true, the resolver adds this input to the required_inputs ledger in the payload and rewrites the rendered Inputs table row to show required=yes with the default nullified. The Setup step then gates execution: if the caller did not supply a value in the invocation prompt, AskUserQuestion is used to gather it. The input's declared default in SKILL.md is treated as inert — it does not satisfy this requirement. If inject_from is also DECLARED, the input is never added to required_inputs (and the table row is never rewritten) regardless of whether the source resolves on this machine: a declared-inject input is supplied by the execution environment (a failed source fails loud there — exit 1 / --inject-only), and keying the ledger off inject SUCCESS would make the payload — and compile-workflow's frozen closure built on it — env-dependent.

allowed_values.description: Restrict caller-supplied value to an enumerated, non-empty, unique list of strings.

inject_from.description: Auto-inject value from an external source. Exactly one source key. Sources EXECUTE at execution time only (the full resolve, or `--inject-only` inside a compiled segment); compile-time resolution passes `--skip-inject` so the compile path never runs them and the compile closure stays env-independent.

file.description: Reads (and trims) file contents. Path relative to config file.

command.description: Runs shell command, uses trimmed stdout. Use sparingly.

default.description: Default value shown in the rendered Inputs table Default column. String only. The resolver substitutes this into the MICROSKILL.md `—` cell at render time. Allowed only in profiles/base.yaml — overlays may not redeclare it (semantic, not schema-enforced).

materialize.description: Marks this as a large / multi-shape input passed BY REFERENCE. When the microskill is invoked directly, the dispatcher normalizes the caller-supplied value — a raw string, a single file path, or a directory of files — into ONE canonical file on disk and supplies only that short path (the body reads it). Avoids args-size truncation of large inline values. The input name should end in `_path`. Only `file` is defined today.

steps.description: Override the SKILL.md '## Steps' section. Numeric string keys (^[0-9]+$) annotate an existing step in place (optional / mandate_tool), keyed by its ORIGINAL number. The reserved keys add/patch/remove restructure the Steps markdown — also keyed by ORIGINAL step number — and are applied REMOVE -> PATCH -> ADD, after which the surviving + added steps are renumbered contiguously. These are markdown-line directives, NOT the deep_merge list-verb engine. A patched/added step whose text introduces branching language (if/else/for each/retry/when…then) is rejected at resolve time; >10 merged steps stays an advisory warning.

remove.description: Original step numbers (>=1) to elide. Renumbering closes the gap. A gate anchored after a removed step falls back to the trailing 'Gates (resolved)' block (it is not silently dropped). A number that names no step in SKILL.md is an error.

patch.description: Replace a step's text in place, keyed by ORIGINAL step number (as a string). The step keeps its position; only its prose changes. A key that names no step in SKILL.md is an error.

text.description: New step body (everything after the renumbered 'N. ' prefix). Internal control flow (branches, loops) is not linted.

add.description: Insert new steps. Applied after remove + patch, in declared order. Each lands after the original step named by 'after' (0 = prepend before the first surviving step). An 'after' that names no original step (other than 0) is an error.

after.description: ORIGINAL step number to insert after; 0 prepends before the first step.

text.description: New step body (everything after the renumbered 'N. ' prefix). Internal control flow (branches, loops) is not linted.

optional.description: When true, runtime skips this step entirely.

mandate_tool.description: Force this step to use a specific tool name (e.g. WebSearch, Bash, Read).

gates.description: Customize validation / approval gates. Existing gates keyed by id (declared in SKILL.md with <!-- gate-id: <id> -->). New gates inserted via add[].

add.description: Insert new gates the skill does not declare.

after.description: Step id or phase id after which the gate fires.

prompt.description: Text shown to the user. Required when type == human_approval.

severity.description: warn = emit warning and continue; hard = block until satisfied.
Soften (warn) or harden (hard) an existing gate.

refs.description: Paths (relative to the config file's directory) read as authoritative reference. Missing path blocks.

name.description: Short kebab/snake-case identifier the skill may use to cite the snippet.

text.description: Short inline authoritative fact (sentence or short paragraph).

extend.description: Path to an existing output artifact to revise in place. Missing path blocks.

runtime.description: Tool & MCP restrictions, and (for workflow composition) the executor agent/model.

allowed_tools.description: Confine the skill to these tool names. Absent -> skill picks tools at discretion.

allowed_mcps.description: MCP server identifiers the skill may query. Absent/empty -> no MCP queries.

agent.description: Agent type (registered under .claude/agents/<name>/) to execute this microskill AS when composed in a workflow `use:` node. The compiler spawns the executor with this agentType so the agent's persona applies. Overridable per profile. Absent -> the generic workflow-subagent runs the body. (Background workflow segments cannot spawn nested agents, so the executor itself must be the intended agent.)

model.description: Model alias the executor runs on in a workflow `use:` node (e.g. 'opus', 'sonnet', 'haiku'). The compiler passes it to the agent() call. Absent -> the segment default (today Sonnet). Set this to pin the agent's intended model, since background segments do not auto-honor the agent's AGENT.md model frontmatter.

output_schema.description: Optional JSON Schema fragment declaring the microskill's structured output contract. When present, the resolver appends a directive instructing the skill to return ONLY a JSON object matching this schema (so the skill emits structured output whether run standalone or composed inside a workflow), and a workflow `use:` node can rely on this shape. Lives in base.yaml as the default contract; a profile overlay may override it wholesale (the resolver REPLACES output_schema across layers — it does not merge — so a profile can declare a domain-specific shape, and a profile that omits it inherits the base default).
